_meta.title: L&T Finance Ltd (NSE: LTF) — Forensic Financial Analysis
_meta.subtitle: FY ended 31-Mar-2026 audited results | results pack 24-Apr-2026 | earnings call 27-Apr-2026
_meta.methodology: Primary-document forensic analysis. Audited figures cross-footed and independently re-parsed with a local LLM (qwen3:14b) - matched to the rupee. Now ANCHORED ON THE FULL FY26 INTEGRATED ANNUAL REPORT (377pp): the ECL Stage 1/2/3 movement, write-offs, overlay/macro-prudential balances, Key Audit Matters, related-party and contingent liabilities are read from the audited notes - the gaps flagged in the results-pack-only draft are now CLOSED. Management claims (transcript) and sell-side (Emkay BUY, ICICI Direct HOLD) triangulated against the audited statements; every figure traced to a source. 7 bull pillars stress-tested by 3 independent adversarial skeptics each.
_meta.redteam_scorecard: 0 of 7 bull pillars fully CONFIRMED; 3 PARTIALLY-CONFIRMED; 4 REFUTED. The central earnings-quality finding is now AUDITED (annual-report notes + a Key Audit Matter), not merely inferred.

verdict_headline: A genuinely transformed, AAA-parent-backed near-pure retail NBFC that is still earning BELOW its cost of capital and well below its own return ambition. FY26 PAT-owners +12.8% is real but LOW-QUALITY: the audited notes show (i) the ENTIRE Rs575cr MFI macro-prudential buffer was unwound to NIL and ~Rs960cr of provisions on existing assets were released, cutting Stage-3 PCR from 73.0% to 68.1% even as total ECL allowance FELL on a +25.6% book; (ii) a flat impairment line that hides a DOUBLED Rs562cr write-off/derecognition loss, with Rs2,413cr of loans charged off to hold GS3 at 2.88% (absolute net Stage-3 actually rose +29%); and (iii) partly inorganic gold-loan growth. Full-year RoA FELL 7bps to 2.37% and the 2.8% target was missed and deferred to Q4FY27. At ~2.6x book on ~11% RoE the stock is priced for an inflection that is promised, not delivered.
stance: NEUTRAL / HOLD-equivalent on fundamentals. Quality franchise, but ~2.6x P/B is unsupported by an ~11% RoE that sits below cost of equity; the re-rating thesis requires a 2.8-3%+ RoA that has not printed and just slipped four quarters. Even the bull (Emkay, TP330) implies only 2.4x FY28E P/B and sees 3% RoA only by FY29E; ICICI Direct is a HOLD (TP325). Constructive case needs: PCR to STOP falling, write-off-adjusted credit cost to fall through a full MFI cycle, and RoA to actually exceed ~2.6%.

red_flags.title: AUDITED: entire Rs575cr MFI macro-prudential buffer unwound to NIL + ~Rs960cr provision release; Stage-3 PCR cut 73.0%->68.1% while total ECL allowance FELL
red_flags.detail: The single largest earnings-quality issue - now confirmed in the audited annual-report notes, not just the call. The ECL note (consolidated Note 45) shows the macro-prudential provision for Rural Group Loans & MFI went from Rs575cr (FY25) to Rs NIL (FY26) - the entire buffer unwound; provisions on existing assets were released by ~Rs960cr (net of recovery); and total ECL allowance FELL to Rs3,458cr from Rs3,537cr DESPITE gross loans growing +25.6% (total ECL coverage 3.72%->2.89%). Computed Stage-3 PCR fell from 72.97% to 68.1%. The CFO confirmed on the call the PCR move 'was nothing but the management overlays... released' and that the company now carries 'no contingency... within the ECL' (could fall further to 60-65%). ECL - and specifically the management overlay - is a formal Key Audit Matter. Releasing coverage on the riskiest (MFI) book in the same year credit cost 'moderated' is a discretionary, non-repeatable lever that flattered FY26 PAT.
red_flags.evidence: AR p.357 footnote (macro-prudential Rs575cr->Nil); AR p.357-358 (ECL allowance 3536.75->3457.84; Stage-3 ECL 2016.49->2061.87 vs gross 2763.32->3027.26 => PCR 72.97%->68.1%; provision release on existing assets -959.60); AR p.235/314 (ECL/overlay is a KAM); transcript L405/L412/L840-842/L945
red_flags.source: annual report Note 45 (p.357-358); KAM p.235; transcript

red_flags.title: 'Flat impairment' masks a DOUBLED write-off/derecognition loss - true credit cost ROSE ~11%
red_flags.detail: Impairment was flat YoY (2,184cr vs 2,193cr) despite +25.6% loan growth - the headline behind 'falling credit cost.' But 'net loss on derecognition under amortised cost' (net write-offs) DOUBLED to 562cr (FY25 275cr). Combined true credit cost rose +11.2% to 2,746cr; on average loans that is 2.60% (combined) vs 2.06% (impairment-only) - a 54bp gap hidden below the impairment line. Normalising impairment to loan growth would cost ~Rs570cr pre-tax, more than the entire ~13.6% PAT-before-exceptional uplift.
red_flags.evidence: audited p.8: impairment 2184.10 vs 2193.35; derecognition 561.77 vs 275.04; combined 2745.87 vs 2468.39 (+11.2%)

red_flags.title: Full-year RoA FELL to 2.37% (down 7bps); the 2.8% target was MISSED and deferred to Q4FY27
red_flags.detail: Management's own CFO disclosure: consolidated FY26 RoA after exceptional = 2.37%, DOWN 7bps YoY (2.39% before exceptional). Orchestrator-derived RoA on avg total assets = 2.27%. Management headlines a Q4 '2.4%, +18bps' point but the clean full-year declined. The MD admits the Lakshya-2026 RoA goal of 2.8-3% was missed ('we achieved 2.4%') and deferred to exit-Q4FY27 ('a lag of about four quarters'). RoA has been ~flat near 2.3-2.4% for years - the inflection is promised, not delivered.
red_flags.evidence: transcript L390-391 (2.37%, -7bps; 2.39% pre-exc), L126 (MD '2.4% +18bps'), L217-221 & L585-588 (target missed, deferred Q4FY27); RoE 11.13%

red_flags.title: AUDITED: Rs2,413cr written off holds GS3 at 2.88% while absolute net Stage-3 rose +29%
red_flags.detail: The annual-report ECL movement confirms Rs2,413cr of loans were charged off in FY26 (FY25 Rs2,382cr) - a ~2% write-off rate that mechanically strips Stage-3 loans off the book and holds reported GS3 at 2.88%. ARC sales were immaterial (127.52cr, sold above book at a gain), so write-offs - not recoveries or cures - are the lever. On +25.6% gross growth, the GS3% fall masks an ABSOLUTE rise in gross Stage-3 +9.5% (2,763->3,027cr) and net Stage-3 +29.3% (746.83->965.39cr). The ratio 'improved' by dilution and charge-off, not credit cure.
red_flags.evidence: AR p.357-358 (write-off 2,413.41 FY26 / 2,382.15 FY25; gross Stage-3 2763.32->3027.26 +9.5%; net Stage-3 746.83->965.39 +29.3%); audited results p.20 (ARC 127.52cr at gain); p.8 (derecognition 561.77cr)
red_flags.source: annual report Note 45 (p.357-358); audited results p.8/20

red_flags.title: NIM+fees FELL 26bps to 10.33%; 'stability' was engineered by funding-cost decline, not the secured/prime mix
red_flags.detail: Full-year consolidated NIM+fees fell to 10.33% from 10.59% (FY25). Pure NIM (ex-fees) fell ~8.8%->8.4% as yield-on-assets dropped ~80bps (15.3%->14.5%); the corridor held only because cost of borrowings fell 7.1%->6.6% in a rate-cut cycle. Management states the shift to secured/prime (mortgages) PRESSURES yields - the opposite of the bull's causal claim - and margin is defended by accelerating higher-yielding gold/micro-LAP/PL. Fees grew only +14.5% vs loans +25.6%, so fee yield is compressing; treasury (net FV gain) collapsed 183->64cr.
red_flags.evidence: transcript L382 (10.33% vs 10.59%), L1137-1148 (mix pressures yields); iDirect NIM 8.8->8.4, yield 15.3->14.5, CoF 7.1->6.6; audited fees 1233.27 vs 1077.34 (+14.5%); net FV gain 64.22 vs 182.64

red_flags.title: Provisioning NOT calibrated to the rising unsecured mix - coverage cut as unsecured book grew +20%
red_flags.detail: The audited notes show unsecured retail loans grew to ~Rs62,013cr (from ~Rs51,579cr, ~+20%) - roughly 53% of the Rs1,16,049cr retail book - yet this is precisely when the macro-prudential buffer was zeroed and Stage-3 PCR cut to ~68%. Management openly states it has NOT yet provided for the unsecured book: 'once we have sufficient provisions created for micro-loan, we may also consider creating provisions for the unsecured portfolio overall.' Peer-group Stage-3 PCRs cited at 50-65%; LTF's 68% 'includes some parts of the overlays' that are being run off. Coverage is being actively reduced into a riskier mix.
red_flags.evidence: AR p.360 (unsecured retail 62,012.51 vs 51,579.46); AR p.357 (macro-prudential ->Nil); transcript L744-756, L549-550, L916-920

red_flags.title: Capital buffer thinning: Tier-2 collapsed, CRAR 18.34%, +25% growth funded entirely by debt
red_flags.detail: Subordinated (Tier-2) liabilities fell 1,833->702cr while RWA grows on +25.6% loans; CRAR 18.34%. The annual report confirms NO equity raise in FY26 - growth was funded entirely by debt (net borrowings +Rs17,641cr; total debt Rs1,09,888cr) - and dividend was held flat at Rs2.75 (vs PAT +13%) to retain capital. Sustaining 20%+ growth + Lakshya 2031 likely needs a future equity raise. LCR 342% confirms ample near-term liquidity (not a funding-risk flag), but the solvency cushion is being consumed.
red_flags.evidence: audited p.9 (subordinated 702.14 vs 1833.29); p.22 (CRAR 18.34%, LCR 342%); AR p.210 (no equity raise; net borrowings +17,640.79cr; debt 1,09,887.69cr)

red_flags.title: Cyclops 'AI edge' is real but tiny, young, unaudited and confounded
red_flags.detail: The quantified edge (2W Cyclops book 30+DPD 2.8% vs industry 7.1%, ~2.5x) rests on an observation book of only ~Rs3,250cr - ~2.8% of the 117,821cr consolidated loans and ~23% of the 14,372cr 2W book - over a 10-month vintage, disclosed only on investor Slide 21 (unaudited; Note 11/15 = no reportable segments, so NO audited segment asset quality). Nostradamus went live in only one product in Nov-25; sub-2% credit cost is an FY28 ambition. Management also attributes outperformance to a 2-year 'prime customer' shift, confounding the AI attribution. The audited combined credit cost still ROSE +11%.
red_flags.evidence: transcript L175-177 (slide 21), L188 (Nostradamus Nov-25), L440 (2W book 14,372cr); iDirect (2.8% vs 7.1%, 3,250cr); audited Note 11/15

red_flags.title: Single-segment disclosure removes audited transparency
red_flags.detail: Note 11/15: 'no separate reportable segments per Ind AS 108.' A diversified lender (Rural/MFI, Farm, 2W, Home/LAP, PL, SME, Gold) reports as ONE segment, so there is NO audited segment-level profitability or asset quality - all AUM-mix, segment-GS3 and the Cyclops numbers are management-reported (unaudited). GS3/NS3/CRAR are also 'Not Applicable at consolidated level' - disclosed only at standalone. Peers (Bajaj/Chola/Shriram) disclose segments.
red_flags.evidence: audited Note 11 (p.11), Note 15 (p.21), Annexure I (p.12 consol 'Not Applicable')

red_flags.title: Part of FY26 growth is inorganic (gold-loan acquisition)
red_flags.detail: Acquired Paul Merchants Finance gold-loan business (09-Jun-2025) for 711.93cr slump sale: ~1,335cr portfolio + goodwill rose 13->196cr. Reported book growth and the new gold yield are partly bought; organic loan growth is below the headline +25.6%.
red_flags.evidence: audited p.11/20: consideration 711.93cr; goodwill 195.79 vs 13.40; portfolio 1335cr

red_flags.title: Residual wholesale/SR tail with a failed resolution and a multi-year drag
red_flags.detail: Project-finance-under-implementation book 1,006cr, of which a resolution plan has FAILED on 218.93cr. Management flags ~Rs7,000cr of security-receipt/wholesale resolution with a 3-4 year tail; the 3.0-3.2% RoA aspiration explicitly EXCLUDES SR gains. Small vs the book but the legacy wind-down is not fully clean.
red_flags.evidence: audited p.20 (PF 1006.07, failed 218.93); transcript L758-760, L774-785

positives.title: Exceptional item is a CONSERVATIVE charge, not a flattering gain
positives.detail: The 28.51cr exceptional (net of tax 21.33cr) is a one-time New Labour Codes past-service-cost provision - it REDUCES reported PAT. Clean PAT-before-exceptional (owners) grew +13.6%. No one-off gains propping up the headline.
positives.evidence: audited p.11; PAT before exceptional owners 3002.51 (+13.6%)

positives.title: Unmodified joint-auditor opinion; exceptional liquidity; AAA-parent funding edge
positives.detail: Joint auditors (Brahmayya & Co + T R Chadha) issued an UNMODIFIED opinion on both consolidated and standalone, no qualification/EoM. LCR 342%. Finance cost grew +12.1% vs book +25.6% - real funding efficiency from the L&T (66% promoter) AAA umbrella.

positives.title: Genuine, largely-executed retail transformation
positives.detail: Retailisation 98% (Lakshya-2026 >95% target beaten), retail book CAGR 28% vs 25% target; NII proxy +14.2% outpacing funding costs. The wholesale-to-retail pivot - the original turnaround thesis - is real and largely done.

positives.title: Forward Stage-1 buffer raised; collections normalising
positives.detail: Stage-1 PCR on the 96% performing book was raised 0.52%->0.80% (a day-1 forward buffer, double the RBI 40bps floor), and management reports collection efficiency back near 99.8% as the MFI cycle recovers. This partly offsets the Stage-3 coverage cut - though it is a forward, not an impaired-book, provision.

positives.title: Clean disclosures elsewhere: small contingent liabilities, transparent related-party, ECL audited as a KAM
positives.detail: The annual report closes the open gaps reassuringly: contingent liabilities are small (~Rs308cr, ~flat YoY) - no hidden off-balance-sheet exposure; related-party dealings with parent L&T are modest and transparent (the Rs157cr brand-license fee is the main item - a cost of the AAA umbrella, not abusive RPT); and ECL (incl. the management overlay) is a formal Key Audit Matter that the joint auditors specifically tested (PD/LGD, staging, macro overlays) before issuing an UNMODIFIED opinion. The aggressive provisioning choices are real but were made within an audited, scrutinised framework.
positives.evidence: AR p.360 (contingent 307.66 vs 326.15); AR p.349 (brand fee 157.10 to L&T); AR p.235/314 (ECL KAM, unmodified)

governance_verdict: IMPORTANT FRAMING: the forensic concerns here are about EARNINGS QUALITY and PROVISIONING DISCRETION - not accounting integrity, controls or compliance. The CARO, Internal Financial Controls, RBI inspection, penalties, ratings and fraud disclosures are all CLEAN. LTF's aggressive FY26 provisioning choices (overlay release, write-off routing, thinning coverage) were made within an audited, regulator-inspected, AAA-rated framework. This is a quality-of-earnings and valuation story, not a fraud or governance story.

governance_controls.item: RBI inspection — divergence in asset classification & provisioning
governance_controls.detail: RBI inspected FY2024-25 and found NO additional provisioning requirement exceeding 5% of reported PBT and NO additional gross NPAs exceeding 5% of reported gross NPAs - i.e., no material divergence. The regulator validated LTF's NPA classification/provisioning. (Caveat: the inspection covered FY25; the FY26 overlay release/PCR cut postdates it.)
No penalties imposed by RBI or any other regulator during FY26 (or FY25).
Joint auditors opine IFC over financial reporting is ADEQUATE and was OPERATING EFFECTIVELY as at 31-Mar-2026 - unqualified, no material weakness.

No default in loan repayment; not declared a wilful defaulter; statutory dues generally regular; s.188/177 (related-party) compliant; adequate internal audit system. Consolidated: component auditors reported NO qualifications/adverse remarks.
governance_controls.source: AR Annexure A p.238-239; consolidated p.317

governance_controls.detail: 165 cases of fraud against the company involving Rs12.47cr (FY25: 136 cases / Rs11.45cr), Rs0.83cr recovered - routine retail-lending frauds (~0.01% of the book), disclosed and provided.
~Rs100cr of legacy VAT/CST, service-tax and GST demands under appeal (FY1996-FY2023 vintages) - immaterial vs the balance sheet.

CARO 3(iii)(d): total amount overdue >90 days = Rs3,509cr as at 31-Mar-2026 - modestly ABOVE the reported gross Stage-3 of Rs3,027cr (a ~Rs480cr gap, likely timing/wholesale-FVTPL), consistent with the write-off-driven staging dynamics.

Long-term AAA (Stable) reaffirmed by all four agencies (CRISIL, CARE, India Ratings, ICRA); short-term A1+. S&P Global UPGRADED LTF to 'BBB' (Aug-2025, in line with the India sovereign upgrade). Confirms the AAA-parent funding edge.

NO equity raise in FY26 and none announced; +25% growth funded ENTIRELY by debt (net borrowings +Rs17,641cr; total debt Rs1,09,888cr). With CRAR at 18.34% and Tier-2 running off, debt-only funding reinforces the capital-consumption watch (F7).

pillars.pillar: Asset quality is STRUCTURALLY improving (GS3 2.88%, credit cost falling)
pillars.summary: Ratios genuinely improved, but 'structural' and 'credit cost falling' fail: combined credit cost ROSE +11%, GS3 is held down by write-offs + denominator growth, and coverage was cut.
pillars.refutation: Combined credit cost (impairment + doubled derecognition) rose +11.2% to 2,746cr (2.60% vs 2.06% of avg loans); PCR cut 74->68% via overlay release; absolute Stage-3 rose.

pillars.pillar: RoA is INFLECTING toward 2.8% near-term / 3.0-3.2% by 2031
pillars.summary: FY26 full-year RoA FELL 7bps to 2.37%; the 2.8% target was missed and deferred to Q4FY27; even bull models reach 3% only by FY29E.
pillars.refutation: Management's own FY26 RoA 2.37% (down 7bps; 2.27% on avg total assets) is ~50bps below the 2.8% bar; the target slipped four quarters.

pillars.pillar: Cyclops/Nostradamus AI underwriting is a DURABLE, quantified edge
pillars.summary: One real quantified data point (2.8% vs 7.1% on a ~3,250cr 2W slice) but unaudited, tiny, 10-month-young, and confounded by a prime-mix shift; FY28 benefit, not realised.
pillars.refutation: No audited segment data (Note 11/15); the edge is ~2.8% of the loan book on a 10-month vintage while audited combined credit cost rose +11%.

pillars.pillar: NIM+fees STABLE/sustainable (~10-10.5%) as book shifts to secured/prime
pillars.summary: Corridor held at ~10.4% but FELL 26bps YoY to 10.33%; stability came from falling funding costs, and the secured/prime shift actually PRESSURES yields (causal claim backwards).
pillars.refutation: Full-year NIM+fees 10.33% vs 10.59%; pure NIM 8.8->8.4%; fees +14.5% vs loans +25.6% (fee yield compressing).

pillars.pillar: FY26 earnings quality is HIGH - clean, core, repeatable
pillars.summary: Growth driven by provisioning levers: flat impairment + doubled write-off routing + a discretionary ~301cr overlay release + 125cr buffer elimination. Treasury/tax were the only clean legs.
pillars.refutation: ~Rs301cr overlay release + Rs125cr macro-buffer subsumed cut PCR 74->68%; normalising provisions to growth (~570cr) exceeds the entire PAT-before-exceptional uplift.

pillars.pillar: Provisioning is ADEQUATE (Stage-3 PCR ~67%) given rising unsecured mix
pillars.summary: The ~68% PCR is the OUTPUT of releasing coverage; the entire Rs575cr MFI macro-prudential buffer was unwound to Nil (audited); total ECL allowance fell on a +25.6% book; management admits the unsecured book is not yet provided for.
pillars.refutation: AUDITED (AR Note 45): macro-prudential Rs575cr->Nil; total ECL allowance 3,537->3,458cr (coverage 3.72%->2.89%); Stage-3 PCR 72.97%->68.1% while unsecured retail grew to ~Rs62,013cr (~53%).

pillars.pillar: The ~2.6x P/B valuation is JUSTIFIED by franchise + forward returns
pillars.summary: At ~11% RoE below a ~13-14% cost of equity, a Gordon test justifies <1x book. 2.6x requires an undelivered ~19% RoE (above mgmt's own 16-18% ceiling). Bulls' own targets imply <=2.4x forward.
pillars.refutation: P/B 2.58x (CMP 288 / BVPS 111.74) on RoE 11.13%; Emkay TP330 implies only 2.4x FY28E; iDirect HOLD at 2.3x FY28E.

mgmt_vs_audited.headline: ~2.4% (MD: +18bps YoY)
mgmt_vs_audited.verified: 2.37% after exceptional, DOWN 7bps (2.39% before); 2.27% on avg total assets
mgmt_vs_audited.read: Headline uses Q4/rounded; clean full-year fell

mgmt_vs_audited.headline: Reported ~2.64% Q4, 'moderating'
mgmt_vs_audited.verified: Combined (impairment+derecognition) 2.60% of avg loans, ROSE +11.2% in absolute Rs

mgmt_vs_audited.headline: 68% 'adequate'
mgmt_vs_audited.verified: Cut from ~74% via Rs290cr overlay release + Rs125cr buffer elimination; could fall to 60-65%

mgmt_vs_audited.headline: Stable ~10-10.5% (Q4 10.47%)
mgmt_vs_audited.read: Held by funding-cost fall, not mix

mgmt_vs_audited.headline: Improved to 2.88%
mgmt_vs_audited.verified: Standalone-only; absolute Stage-3 rose ~10%; flattered by write-offs + growth

sellside.note: 3% RoA only by FY29E; concedes Q4 credit cost beat 'led by ECL refresh'; PCR -464bps QoQ to 67.3%
RoA flat-to-down in model; 2.8% RoA 'delayed to Q4FY27 due to MFI crisis'; HOLD

valuation.gordon_note: Justified P/B = (RoE - g)/(Ke - g). At RoE ~11.1%, Ke ~13-14%, g ~8-12% the justified multiple is <1.0x (franchise value-neutral-to-destructive since RoE < Ke). Sustaining 2.6x requires a durable ~19% RoE - above management's own 16-18% Lakshya-2031 ceiling. The multiple already capitalises a best-case 2031 outcome.
valuation.read: Priced for a delivered inflection that is still promised. Downside protection is the franchise + AAA parent; upside requires RoA to actually exceed ~2.6% and coverage to stop falling.

data_integrity_log: Audited consolidated P&L independently re-parsed with qwen3:14b -> matched orchestrator extraction to the rupee on all 13 line items.
All consolidated statements cross-foot exactly (total income, total expenses, PBT, PAT, total debt, networth); DuPont reconciles (RoA 2.27% x 4.90 leverage = 11.13% RoE).
The two 27MB PDFs (consolidated-results / standalone-results) are BYTE-IDENTICAL (MD5 f941cf96) - one mislabeled; the single file contains both entity levels. No data missing.

screener cons_6417 / stand_6417 use a generic MANUFACTURING template (Raw Material, Inventory Days, Power & Fuel, EV/EBITDA, OPM%) force-fit on an NBFC -> those ratios are INVALID for a lender and were discarded; only PAT/equity/borrowings/total assets used for long-horizon trend.
Sell-side rating CORRECTION: ICICI Direct is HOLD (TP 325), not BUY; only Emkay is BUY (TP 330). Both are below today's trailing 2.6x on a forward basis.
GS3/NS3/CRAR are 'Not Applicable at consolidated level' (audited p.12); RBI asset-quality ratios disclosed only at standalone (p.22). Sell-side loosely labels 2.88%/0.96% 'consolidated.'
Management's RoA framing is internally inconsistent: MD '2.4%, +18bps' (Q4/rounded) vs CFO '2.37%, down 7bps' (clean full-year).
Overlay-release, PCR 74%->68%, RoA 2.37%, NIM+fees 10.33% vs 10.59%, Cyclops slice and all sell-side estimates were re-verified by direct grep of the primary text before inclusion.

GAPS NOW CLOSED with the full FY26 Integrated Annual Report (377pp): the ECL Stage 1/2/3 movement, write-offs (Rs2,413cr), the macro-prudential unwind (Rs575cr->Nil), Key Audit Matters (ECL + IT, unmodified), related-party (L&T brand fee Rs157cr) and contingent liabilities (Rs308cr) are now read from the audited notes. The central earnings-quality finding is AUDITED, and is in fact LARGER than the transcript implied (full-year macro-prudential release Rs575cr vs the Rs125cr Q4 slice).

Audited ECL movement cross-checks: Stage-3 ECL 2016.49->2061.87 on gross 2763.32->3027.26 => PCR 72.97%->68.1%; total allowance 3536.75->3457.84 (fell on +25.6% gross); macro-prudential Rs575cr->Nil (RGL & MFI). Consistent across standalone (p.290) and consolidated (p.357) notes.
monitorables: Stage-3 PCR direction - does coverage STOP falling (mgmt flags possible 60-65%) as unsecured mix rises?
Write-off-adjusted credit cost (impairment + derecognition) through a full MFI cycle - is the ~2.6% trending down, or just a favourable cycle + overlay release?
RoA glide-path - does it actually print above ~2.6% (deferred 2.8% Q4FY27 milestone) vs the delivered 2.37%?
Capital - CRAR trajectory and any equity raise as Tier-2 runs off and RWA grows 20%+
Quality/recurrence of the 1,233cr fee line (compressing vs loan growth) and the ~7,000cr SR/wholesale resolution tail
Organic vs inorganic AUM growth ex the Paul Merchants gold acquisition

sources.role: PRIMARY audited anchor — ECL movement, KAMs, related-party, contingent liabilities
